div.p: William by the grace of God king of the Scots sends greeting to all worthy men of his land, French, English and Scots. Let both those to come as well as those present know me to have granted and by this my charter to have made firm to the church of St Mary at Melrose and to the monks serving God in that place the donation that Earl Cospatric has made to the same church and monks by the agreement and permission of his heir in respect of the land Hartside and Spot, and of a saltpan, for the salvation of his soul and the souls of his predecessors, in all things so freely and quietly in everlasting alms, as freely and quietly the charter of Cospatric himself for them bears witness. Witness Richard bishop of St Andrews, Ingram bishop of Glasgow, John abbot of Kelso, Osbert abbot of Jedburgh, Nicholas the chancellor, Matthew the archdeacon of St Andrews, Richard de Morville, Walter son of Alan, Walter Corbet, Robert Avenel, Gilbert son of Richer, Philip de Volognes, Bernard son of Brian, Robert son of Guy, Ralph de Clare, William de Mortimer, Richard the chaplain, Imer the clerk, William de la Haye, Ralph le Nain, Walter of Barclay. At Lanark.
